---
title: "STAT 4355 Project"
author: "Sejal Patel"
date: "2024-04-17"
output: pdf_document
---
## STAT 4355 Project
### Group: Snooze Stats

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
if (!require(ggpubr)) install.packages("ggpubr")
library(ggpubr)
library(viridis)


sleep_data <- read.csv("sleep.csv")

# ***************************************************
#                   CLEANING DATA                   *
# ***************************************************

# Getting rid of PersonID column
sleep <- select(sleep_data, -1)
# View(sleep)

# Renaming columns
colnames(sleep) <- c("Gender", "Age", "Occupation", "Sleep_Duration", "Sleep_Quality", "Physical_Activity", "Stress_Level", "BMI_Category", "BP", "Heart_Rate", "Daily_Steps", "Sleep_Disorder")

# Changing "Normal Weight" to "Normal" in BMI column
sleep$BMI_Category[which(sleep$BMI_Category == "Normal Weight")] <- "Normal"

# checking for NAs
sum(is.na(sleep))
# _____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

# Convert Age to categorical variable 
sleep$Age_Group <- cut(sleep$Age, breaks = c(0, 18, 35, 50, 65, 100), labels = c("Under 18", "18-35", "35-50", "50-65", "65+"))

# ------------------------------------ BAR PLOTS ------------------------------------ 
# Bar Plot for Gender vs. Sleep Duration
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Gender, y = Sleep_Duration, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
  labs(title = "Average Sleep Duration by Gender", x = "Gender", y = "Average Sleep Duration (hours)")


# Bar Plot for Occupation vs. Sleep Duration
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Occupation, y = Sleep_Duration, fill = Occupation)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +  
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +  # Use the 'D' option for discrete color scale
  labs(title = "Average Sleep Duration by Occupation", x = "Occupation", y = "Average Sleep Duration (hours)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))




# ------------------------------------ SCATTER PLOTS ------------------------------------ 
# Scatter Plot for Age vs. Sleep Duration
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Age, y = Sleep_Duration)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(title = "Sleep Duration vs Age", x = "Age", y = "Sleep Duration (hours)")

# Scatter Plot for Age vs. Sleep Quality
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Age, y = Sleep_Quality)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(title = "Sleep Quality vs Age", x = "Age", y = "Sleep Quality")


# ------------------------------------ HISTOGRAMS ------------------------------------ 
# Histogram for Age
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "cornflowerblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Age", x = "Age", y = "Frequency")

# Histogram for Sleep Duration based on age and gender
ggplot(sleep, aes(x=Age, fill= Gender)) +
  geom_bar() +
    labs (title = "Histogram of Sleep Duration on Age & Gender", x = "Age", y = "Frequency") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,40), breaks = seq(0, 40, 10)) 



# ------------------------------------ BOX PLOTS ------------------------------------ 
# Boxplot for Sleep Duration by Gender
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Gender, y = Sleep_Duration, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Sleep Duration by Gender", x = "Gender", y = "Sleep Duration (hours)")

# Boxplot for Sleep Quality by Gender
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Gender, y = Sleep_Quality, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Sleep Quality by Gender", x = "Gender", y = "Sleep Quality")

# Turn age into a categorible variables by setting age ranges (under 20, 30-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60+) and use this data to add to the box plot 
# Use a function to categorize each observation into an age range and make the box plot

# Categorizing age into groups
sleep <- sleep %>%
  mutate(Age_Group = cut(Age,
                         breaks = c(-Inf, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, Inf),
                         labels = c("Under 20", "20-29", "30-39", "40-49", "50-59", "60+"),
                         right = FALSE))

# Creating a box plot for Sleep Duration by Age Group
ggplot(sleep, aes(x = Age_Group, y = Sleep_Duration, fill = Age_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Box Plot of Sleep Duration by Age Group",
       x = "Age Group",
       y = "Sleep Duration (hours)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  
        legend.position = "none") 

ggplot(sleep, aes(x = reorder(Occupation, -Sleep_Duration, FUN = mean), y = Sleep_Duration, fill = Occupation)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +  # Use the 'D' option for discrete color scale
  labs(title = "Average Sleep Duration by Occupation", x = "Occupation", y = "Average Sleep Duration (hours)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

qq_plot <- function(data, variable, title) {
  qq_data <- qqnorm(data[[variable]], plot.it = FALSE)
  df <- data.frame(Theoretical = qq_data$x, Sample = qq_data$y)
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(sample = Sample)) +
    stat_qq() +
    stat_qq_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +  # Update to 'linewidth'
    labs(title = title, x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
    theme_minimal()
  return(p)
}

# Create QQ plots
qq_sleep_duration <- qq_plot(sleep, "Sleep_Duration", "QQ Plot for Sleep Duration")

ggarrange(qq_sleep_duration)





```

```{r}
sleep <- sleep %>%
  mutate(Age_Group = cut(Age,
                         breaks = c(-Inf, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, Inf),
                         labels = c("Under 20", "20_29", "30_39", "40_49", "50_59", "60+"),
                         right = FALSE),
         Gender = as.factor(Gender),
         Occupation = as.factor(Occupation),
         BMI_Category = as.factor(BMI_Category))

# Blood Pressure might need to be split or used as is, depending on how it's currently formatted.
# Assuming 'BP' is something like '120/80', we'll split it and convert to numeric:
sleep <- sleep %>%
  separate(BP, into = c("Systolic_BP", "Diastolic_BP"), sep = "/", convert = TRUE)

# Build the model
model <- lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Gender + Age_Group + Occupation + Physical_Activity + 
            Stress_Level + BMI_Category + Systolic_BP + Diastolic_BP + Heart_Rate + Daily_Steps, data = sleep)

# Fit the full model
full_model <- lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Gender + Age_Group + Occupation + 
                 Physical_Activity + Stress_Level + BMI_Category + 
                 Systolic_BP + Diastolic_BP + Heart_Rate + Daily_Steps, data = sleep)

# View the summary to decide which variables to remove
summary(full_model)





# Automated stepwise model selection based on AIC
stepwise_model <- step(full_model, direction = "both")

# View the summary of the stepwise-selected model
summary(stepwise_model)





```





```
